newsletter.markdown:
**News**

**[STAT+: White House offers its science blueprint: More AI, less life sciences](https://www.statnews.com/2026/07/24/science-new-golden-age-report-draws-mixed-reaction/?utm_campaign=rss)** — STAT  
- Trump administration blueprint proposes shifting hundreds of billions in research dollars from universities to industry.  
- Framed as a “new golden age” for U.S. science; draws mixed reactions from research community.

**[Senate advances MAHA-friendly bill for food warning labels and junk food ad bans](https://www.statnews.com/2026/07/24/ultraprocessed-food-warning-label-bill-clears-senate-committee-bipartisan-vote/?utm_campaign=rss)** — STAT  
- Bipartisan Senate committee vote advances legislation targeting ultra-processed foods with warning labels and advertising restrictions.  
- Reflects growing “MAHA” (Make America Healthy Again) influence on food policy.

**[Opinion: The U.S. pharmaceutical supply chain problem is not about China](https://www.statnews.com/2026/07/24/pharmaceutical-supply-chain-resilience-china/?utm_campaign=rss)** — STAT  
- Argues national-security framing around China distracts from structural vulnerabilities in API and generic manufacturing.  
- Calls for resilience strategies focused on domestic capacity and diversified sourcing.

**[Scribe secures $129M in a rare gene editing IPO](https://www.biopharmadive.com/news/scribe-ipo-price-gene-editing-biotech-pcsk9-lpa/825933/)** — BioPharma Dive  
- Scribe Therapeutics priced 8.58M shares at $15 (ticker: SCTX), first gene-editing IPO since Metagenomi (Feb 2024).  
- Epigenetic silencing platform (no DNA cleavage) targeting common cardiometabolic diseases: lead STX-1150 (PCSK9) in Phase 1, data expected H1 2027; LPA and APOC3 programs preclinical, INDs targeted 2027–2028.  
- Positions as longer-lasting alternative to chronic injectables/orals for adherence-heavy indications.

**Regulatory**

**[FDA panel endorses broader use of certain peptides](https://www.biopharmadive.com/news/fda-peptides-advisory-committee-vote-bpc-kpv-tb-mots/826062/)** — BioPharma Dive  
- Advisory committee narrowly voted to add four peptides to the 503A Bulks List (compounding ingredients): KPV and TB-500 (wound healing); BPC-157 (ulcerative colitis); MOTS-c (weight loss).  
- Peptides lack rigorous efficacy/safety data; banned in 2021 but widely used via grey market and promoted by HHS Secretary RFK Jr.  
- Panel split on precedent: supporters cite regulating existing demand; dissenters warn of market-driven decisions over evidence. FDA to consider votes; three more peptides (Emideltide, Semax, Epitalon) reviewed Friday.

**Papers & Preprints**

**[From physics-based intelligence to molecular design: linking mechanistic learning across scales](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/42493707/)** — PubMed  
- Review/overview; body text limited to title. Describes framework connecting physics-based models to molecular design across scales.

**[Immunostimulatory lipogel implant enhances cancer immunotherapy](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/42493626/)** — PubMed  
- Primary research; body text limited to title. Reports lipogel implant boosting anti-tumor immunity.

**[A novel small molecule remyelination therapy for multiple sclerosis](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/42493540/)** — PubMed  
- Primary research; body text limited to title. Identifies small-molecule candidate promoting remyelination in MS models.

**[In silico pipeline for GSK-3β inhibitor discovery in Alzheimer's disease using pharmacophore screening, docking, ADME filtering, and MD validation](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/42493523/)** — PubMed  
- Computational study; body text limited to title. Describes virtual screening workflow yielding GSK-3β inhibitor candidates.

**[Supramolecular Recognition-Based Hydrogels Incorporating VEGF and Hesperadin for Infarcted Myocardial Repair and Angiogenesis](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/42492872/)** — PubMed  
- Primary research; body text limited to title. Dual-factor hydrogel for post-MI cardiac repair.

**[Artificial intelligence-based methods and applications in clinical and diagnostic microbiology: Current challenges and future perspectives](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/42492779/)** — PubMed  
- Review/overview; body text limited to title. Surveys AI in microbiology diagnostics.

**[AI-assisted design of next-generation antibiotics against multidrug-resistant Staphylococcus aureus](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/42492738/)** — PubMed  
- Primary research; body text limited to title. AI-driven antibiotic discovery targeting MRSA.

**[Brain corticogenesis promotes SARS-CoV-2 neuro-glial tropism through lipid-dependent viral replication](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/42492515/)** — PubMed  
- Primary research; body text limited to title. Links developmental lipid metabolism to SARS-CoV-2 neurotropism.

**[BRD4: From molecular understanding to therapeutics development](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/42492506/)** — PubMed  
- Review/overview; body text limited to title. Covers BRD4 biology and inhibitor development.

**[Cannabinoid CB1 allosteric ligands suppress inflammatory pain in male mice without tolerance, motor impairment, memory deficits or respiratory depression](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/42492483/)** — PubMed  
- Primary research; body text limited to title. Preclinical profile of CB1 allosteric modulators for pain.

**[Nanoparticle Biofunctionalization of Bioprosthetic Heart Valves to Modulate Mechanisms of Structural Valve Deterioration](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/42492292/)** — PubMed  
- Primary research; body text limited to title. Nanoparticle surface modification to improve valve durability.

**[PLIN3 knockdown enhances T cell-mediated cytotoxicity in non-small cell lung cancer via autophagy-dependent PD-L1 degradation](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/42492236/)** — PubMed  
- Primary research; body text limited to title. Identifies PLIN3 as regulator of PD-L1 turnover and T-cell response in NSCLC.

**X / Twitter**

**[Recapping those Epcore DLBCL-1 endpoints after yesterday's strange PR from $GMAB](https://x.com/JacobPlieth/status/2080539088810119498)** — Jacob Plieth (@JacobPlieth)  
- Thread recaps Epcore DLBCL-1 trial endpoints presented at EHA 2026 following Genmab/AbdV press release.  
- Highlights discrepancies between disclosed endpoints and prior protocol; relevant for epcoritamab (DuoBody CD3×CD20) in relapsed/refractory DLBCL.

**[Can anyone think of any other examples of a clinical trial whose primary endpoints differed by region?](https://x.com/JacobPlieth/status/2080555684458791153)** — Jacob Plieth (@JacobPlieth)  
- Open question soliciting precedents for region-specific primary endpoints in global trials.  
- Signals regulatory/design complexity for multinational development programs.

**Worth watching:** FDA’s final decision on 503A peptide listings (expected after Friday’s panel session) will set precedent for compounding regulation vs. wellness-market demand.

podcast.topic: Covalent inhibitors: reversible vs irreversible warheads, selectivity, and the KRAS G12C story

youtube.markdown:
- Pure Science
    - [How Fast Is the Universe Really Expanding? | PODCAST: The Joy of Why](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Srcz66KUDbY) -- Quanta Magazine
        - Adam Riess traces his path from graduate student to Nobel laureate, detailing how Type Ia supernovae serve as standardizable candles for measuring cosmic distances via the distance ladder (Cepheid variables, tip of the red giant branch, and supernovae in the same host galaxies).
        - The 1998 discovery that distant supernovae were dimmer than expected revealed the universe’s expansion is accelerating, reviving Einstein’s cosmological constant as the leading explanation for dark energy within the ΛCDM model.
        - The Hubble tension now centers on a ~9% discrepancy: the local distance-ladder measurement yields H₀ ≈ 73 km/s/Mpc, while the CMB-inferred ΛCDM value is ≈ 67 km/s/Mpc — a difference that exceeds 5σ and cannot be explained by known systematic errors.
        - Riess outlines how new telescopes (JWST, Roman, ground-based ELTs) are targeting multiple independent distance anchors — Cepheids, TRGB, JAGB stars, and gravitational-wave standard sirens — to cross-check the distance ladder and test whether early-universe physics or new dark-energy dynamics resolve the tension.
        - The tension may indicate physics beyond ΛCDM, such as early dark energy, neutrino self-interactions, or a running Planck mass, but Riess emphasizes that exhaustive systematic checks on the distance-ladder side continue before invoking new cosmology.
